user_message: Just came out of security posture with Blue Yonder Airlines. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 14:55 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at Blue Yonder Airlines

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-01-13 12:32 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our CIO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Blue Yonder Airlines: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Cross-Sell Trigger Email template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
